behavior_summary: This script implements a simple world object: examining the object (U8P_FALLROCK::look) displays a short description 'rock' and then the U8P_FALLROCK::look process yields.

A helper process U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit is defined to handle the object being struck: it appears to spawn a short-lived helper/cleanup process and then invoke Item::hurl to make the rock be hurled/thrown.
Behavior is self-contained: no global flags are read or written; interactions are driven by engine events (U8P_FALLROCK::look/examine and hit) and by spawning/Item calls local to the object.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FALLROCK::look is a process that immediately issues a single bark ('rock') and then suspends/yields — a straight-line describe-and-end flow (confidence: high).

U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit spawns an external helper process (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) and then calls process Item::hurl on this object; both external calls are suspended around — inferred sequential flow with waits (confidence: medium).
No conditional branching or menu/dialogue flow is present; most non-bark nodes in the raw are low-confidence remnants.
open_questions: What exact engine event maps to U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit (physical collision, weapon hit, or other damage hook)? (confidence: low)
What precisely does the spawned FREE::func1C19 helper do (visual effect, resource cleanup, delayed deletion)? (confidence: low)
What are the concrete meanings of the two parameters (uword, word) passed into U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit in gameplay terms (damage, source id, direction, impulse)? (confidence: low)
Are there any other scripts or systems that listen for the Item::hurl result or the spawned PID (pid local) to perform follow-up actions? (confidence: low)

main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines/looks at the rock; display the text 'rock' and then suspend/end the U8P_FALLROCK::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player inspects/examines the object (engine U8P_FALLROCK::look/examine action).
Inferred: tied to the object's U8P_FALLROCK::look/examine trigger region / fast-area entry for U8P_FALLROCK::look events.

function_map.role: Examine/describe handler that barks a short description ('rock') and ends the process.

function_map.name: U8P_FALLROCK::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle being hit: spawn a helper/cleanup process and call Item::hurl to make the rock fly/hurl; declared params (uword, word).

external_calls.purpose: Spawn a short-lived helper/cleanup process (likely for cleanup, freeing, or a small visual effect). Return PID is captured into a local (pid).
Cause this item to be hurled/thrown using parameters inferred as (0x04, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, this) in the raw — effectively makes the rock fly away or react physically to the hit.
